title: anadenanthera colubrina var. colubrina
intro: Anadenanthera colubrina var. colubrina is a variety of the Anadenanthera colubrina tree, native to the tropical regions of South America, also known as the "Yopo" tree due to its seeds being used as a stimulant. The seeds of this tree contain the alkaloids harmine and DMT, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal and psychoactive properties. Anadenanthera colubrina var. colubrina is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to stimulate the nervous system, increase alertness, and aid in the treatment of various conditions such as anxiety, depression, and even some types of cancer. The seeds are typically prepared as a snuff or tea, and are also used in traditional medicine in various herbal preparations, often in combination with other plants to enhance their effects.
